name: Merton model - Financial Engineering
snippet: We will implement the Merton (1976) jump diffusion model in Python using Monte Carlo methods. Then, we will discuss the comparison with the Black-Scholes framework and the meaning and interpretation of the different parameters.

name: Merton Jump Diffusion Model Explained: Option Pricing with
snippet: Learn how the Merton Jump Diffusion Model improves option pricing by incorporating sudden market jumps and volatility changes. Explore jump risk, model assumptions, formulas, and comparisons to the Black-Scholes model in options trading.

name: Monte Carlo Methods for Options Pricing: A Complete 2026
snippet: Monte Carlo easily adapts to models beyond standard GBM, including stochastic volatility (Heston model), jump-diffusion (Merton jump-diffusion model for assets with sudden price gaps), and fractional Brownian motion for assets with long-term memory effects.

name: NilayGanvit/heston-merton-option-pricing - GitHub
snippet: This repository contains the implementation of advanced option pricing models. The focus is on extending beyond the Black-Scholes framework to incorporate stochastic volatility (Heston model) and jump diffusion (Merton model).

name: Beyond Black-Scholes: A Computational Framework for Option
snippet: In this research, four different mathematical models: ‘Monte Carlo Method’, ‘Monte Carlo Method with Merton Jump Diffusion Model’, ‘Monte Carlo Method with GARCH’ and Heston Model have been developed or estimating option pricing.

name: GitHub - eliaceolini/option-pricing-models: This project
snippet: This project implements and compares three advanced option pricing frameworks widely used in quantitative finance: The goal is to analyze their ability to reproduce implied volatility surfaces and to assess their impact on pricing both vanilla and exotic derivatives.

name: Jump-Diffusion Option Pricing Calculator | MetricGate
snippet: Use our Jump-Diffusion Option Pricing Calculator to price options under jump risk, compare against the pure-diffusion benchmark, and visualise the implied volatility smile generated by the jump component.
